questions.question: What is the main thread in a Java application and how is it created?
keyPoints: The main thread is the initial thread that begins execution when a Java program starts.
It is created automatically by the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) when the program begins running.
The main thread begins execution with the `main()` method, which serves as its entry point.

questions.question: How can you obtain a reference to the main thread object in Java?
keyPoints: A reference to the main Thread object can be obtained by calling `Thread.currentThread()` from within the `main()` method.
The reference can be stored and used to control the thread, such as by checking its name or changing its priority.
Example code: `Thread mainThread = Thread.currentThread();`

questions.question: Compare and contrast the main thread with other threads in a Java application.
keyPoints: The main thread is created automatically by the JVM, while other threads are created by the application using the `Thread` class or `Runnable` interface.
The main thread begins execution with the `main()` method, while other threads begin execution with their own `run()` method.
The main thread terminates when the `main()` method returns or when its `run()` method completes, while other threads terminate when their `run()` method completes.
The main thread has a default priority of 5 (NORM_PRIORITY), while other threads have a default priority of 5 (NORM_PRIORITY) unless specified otherwise.
The main thread can create and start additional threads using the `Thread` class or `Runnable` interface.

questions.question: Explain how to control the main thread in Java, including setting its priority, name, and putting it to sleep.
keyPoints: The main thread can be controlled using various methods, including `setPriority()`, `setName()`, and `sleep()`.
Example code: `Thread mainThread = Thread.currentThread(); mainThread.setPriority(Thread.MAX_PRIORITY); mainThread.setName("MyMainThread"); mainThread.sleep(1000);`
The `setPriority()` method sets the priority of the main thread, while the `setName()` method sets its name.
The `sleep()` method puts the main thread to sleep for a specified period of time.
The `getId()` method returns the unique identifier of the main thread.

questions.question: Discuss the role of the main thread in multithreading and its potential limitations.
keyPoints: The main thread plays a crucial role in multithreading, serving as the entry point for the application and creating other threads.
The main thread can be used effectively in multithreading by creating other threads to perform specific tasks and controlling their execution.
Potential limitations of the main thread include its default priority and potential for blocking or termination.
The main thread should be used judiciously in multithreading to ensure efficient and effective execution of the application.
